title: Large US banks report record profits in second quarter
summary: JPMorgan, Citigroup, Bank of America and Goldman Sachs see profit increases between 27% and 78%, driven by investment banking and trading.

title: US-Iran tensions drive up oil and trigger airstrikes in Gulf
summary: Crude surpasses $87 after confrontation in Strait of Hormuz, as Trump withdraws 20% toll and US strikes Iranian missiles targeting oil tankers.

title: IBM plummets 25% on warning of lower revenue from shift to AI
summary: The company anticipates second-quarter results worse than expected, triggering its biggest-ever stock drop and dragging down the software sector.

title: US inflation falls to 3.5% in June, steepest drop in six years, and global stock markets rally
summary: US CPI moderates more than expected, boosting the S&P 500, Nasdaq, and European markets despite the drag from IBM.

title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh says central bank will not tolerate high inflation
summary: In his first appearance before Congress, Warsh states the Federal Reserve will act firmly to control prices.

title: ECB selects 36 providers for digital euro pilot, includes critics in project
summary: The European Central Bank advances the digital currency with a pilot program involving 36 entities while seeking to balance the inclusion of critical voices.

title: Cybersecurity and AI chip stocks lead Wall Street gains as CrowdStrike rallies
summary: CrowdStrike and Monolithic rise more than 10%, contrasting with the software sector collapse after IBM's debacle.

title: Warren Buffett cuts donations to Gates Foundation, plans to give away all Berkshire holdings
summary: The investor ends contributions following Epstein revelations and announces he will transfer all his Berkshire Hathaway wealth by 2034.

title: LegalZoom and Sea Limited executives sell large stock packages
summary: LegalZoom's legal director sells more than 50,000 shares, while Sea Limited's COO divests 40,000 shares for $4.5 million.

title: Samsung accelerates chip plant construction in Yongin, explores US listing
summary: The South Korean company advances the opening of the first of six plants to 2029 and considers issuing ADRs in the US market after an initial rejection.

title: US small-cap stocks surge as investors rotate away from large tech
summary: Interest in small caps grows on Wall Street, with notable gains in the sector.

title: Target faces structural problems deeper than believed
summary: The retail chain grapples with deep challenges affecting its performance beyond inflation and competition.

title: BMW's US business proves key to its current performance
summary: North American sales significantly contribute to the German automaker's performance in a complicated global environment.

title: Next phase of AI rally begins, analysts say
summary: The AI sector may enter a new bullish phase as companies begin monetizing the technology.

title: Evotec cuts financial forecasts, stock plummets
summary: The German biotech company lowers its revenue outlook, triggering a sharp market drop.

title: Swiss platform Bitcoin Suisse begins operations in Germany
summary: The cryptocurrency exchange expands into the German market, offering custody and trading services.

title: Yield on Spanish 9-month Treasury bills reaches 2.63%, highest since 2024
summary: Short-term Spanish public debt interest rises to highs not seen since November 2024.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi leads Liberal Democratic Party membership drive in Japan
summary: In the LDP internal race, Takaichi secures the highest number of new members, followed by lawmaker Aoyama.

title: Spanish Congress rejects government's budget stability path
summary: The opposition blocks the fiscal trajectory proposed by the executive, complicating the drafting of the general budget.

title: ARC Resources shareholders approve Shell's $16.4 billion acquisition
summary: The acquisition deal receives investor green light, boosting Shell's expansion in the gas sector.
